prompt: The class does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: static methods cannot access instance fields even of the same class
This compiles correctly — private members are accessible anywhere within the same class, including static methods
obj.secret requires a cast to int before printing
Access.secret must be static to be accessed from a static method
explanation: Private access in Java is class-level, not instance-level. A static method in the same class can access private fields of any instance of that class. This code compiles and prints 42. Access control is checked by the compiler based on the class boundary, not whether the method is static.
